items.title: Default to Express for tidy DataFrame EDA
items.avoid: Building per-class go.Scatter loops before trying px.scatter with color=.
items.prefer: px.scatter / px.histogram / px.line with x, y, color, hover_data, and facet_col for small multiples.
items.why: Express encodes the same patterns as Seaborn with less code and built-in legend and hover wiring.
items.example: px.scatter(df, x='pc1', y='pc2', color='cluster', hover_data=['doc_id']) for embedding previews.

items.title: Configure hover for ML traceability
items.avoid: Publishing interactive plots that only show x and y on hover.
items.prefer: hover_data for Express; customdata + hovertemplate for GO with ids, labels, predictions, and split.
items.why: Interactivity pays off when stakeholders can identify which observation they are seeing.

items.title: Use shared axes on training metric subplots
items.avoid: Independent x-axes on loss and accuracy panels when both are epoch-indexed.
items.prefer: make_subplots(..., shared_xaxes=True) and hovermode='x unified' on the combined layout.
items.why: Aligned epochs make cross-metric inspection and overfitting diagnosis faster.
items.example: Row 1: train/val loss; row 2: train/val F1; shared epoch axis with unified hover.

items.title: Match export format to audience
items.avoid: Screenshotting the notebook for collaborators who need to explore points.
items.prefer: fig.write_html for interactive handoff; Matplotlib savefig when the deliverable is a static report figure.
items.why: HTML preserves zoom and hover; PNG from Plotly is optional and non-interactive.
items.example: write_html('error_analysis.html', include_plotlyjs='cdn') after building the diagnostic scatter.
